# Trail-pct walk-fold validation

**Read-only research; dry-run; no production changes.** Generated by `.claude/local_scripts/backtest_trail_walkfold.py`.

- **Window total**: 2025-04-05 → 2026-05-07 (funding-data floor 2025-04-05 enforced by harness)
- **Folds**: 6 non-overlapping ~2-month sub-windows (matching Phase-1A walkfold monthly structure, paired into 2-month folds)
- **Trail values swept**: [0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3]
- **Other axes (held at deployed values)**: horizon=10d, policy=D, selection=top_n/n=6, activation_pct=0.01, post-parabolic filter ON
- **Total backtests**: 36 (6 folds × 6 trail values), elapsed 4.7s

## Pairwise dominance counts (out of 6 folds)

"X dominates Y in fold F" iff Sharpe(X) > Sharpe(Y) AND |MaxDD(X)| < |MaxDD(Y)| in fold F. Decision rule: X dominates Y if dominance count ≥ 4 / 6.

| X \ Y | 0.05 | 0.10 | 0.15 | 0.20 | 0.25 | 0.30 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **0.05** | — | 6–0–0 ✓ | 6–0–0 ✓ | 6–0–0 ✓ | 6–0–0 ✓ | 6–0–0 ✓ |
| **0.10** | 0–6–0 | — | 6–0–0 ✓ | 6–0–0 ✓ | 6–0–0 ✓ | 5–0–1 ✓ |
| **0.15** | 0–6–0 | 0–6–0 | — | 6–0–0 ✓ | 5–0–1 ✓ | 5–0–1 ✓ |
| **0.20** | 0–6–0 | 0–6–0 | 0–6–0 | — | 4–1–1 ✓ | 4–1–1 ✓ |
| **0.25** | 0–6–0 | 0–6–0 | 0–5–1 | 1–4–1 | — | 3–1–2 |
| **0.30** | 0–6–0 | 0–5–1 | 0–5–1 | 1–4–1 | 1–3–2 | — |

Cell entry `a–b–c`: X wins in `a` folds, Y wins in `b` folds, neither in `c` folds (ties or mixed). ✓ = X dominates Y by the ≥4-of-6 rule.

## Deployed (trail=0.30) vs candidates

| candidate | folds where Sharpe(c) > Sharpe(0.30) | folds where |MaxDD(c)| < |MaxDD(0.30)| | folds where BOTH (=dominance) | verdict |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| trail=0.05 | 6 / 6 | 6 / 6 | 6 / 6 | **DOMINATES** |
| trail=0.10 | 6 / 6 | 5 / 6 | 5 / 6 | **DOMINATES** |
| trail=0.15 | 6 / 6 | 5 / 6 | 5 / 6 | **DOMINATES** |
| trail=0.20 | 5 / 6 | 4 / 6 | 4 / 6 | **DOMINATES** |
| trail=0.25 | 5 / 6 | 3 / 6 | 3 / 6 | weak |

## Rank-order check: does 0.10 > 0.15 > 0.20 > 0.30 hold per fold?

Counts the number of folds in which the (Sharpe-based) rank-order matches the late-fold expectation.

| fold | Sharpe @ 0.10 | @ 0.15 | @ 0.20 | @ 0.30 | 0.10>0.15? | 0.15>0.20? | 0.20>0.30? | full chain? |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| F1_2025-04-05_2025-06-04 | 11.39 | 9.78 | 9.60 | 9.76 | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| F2_2025-06-05_2025-08-04 | 6.83 | 6.43 | 6.22 | 5.51 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** |
| F3_2025-08-05_2025-10-04 | 5.28 | 5.07 | 4.94 | 4.81 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** |
| F4_2025-10-05_2025-12-04 | 6.98 | 6.57 | 6.25 | 5.62 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** |
| F5_2025-12-05_2026-02-04 | 4.31 | 4.00 | 3.88 | 3.32 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** |
| F6_2026-02-05_2026-05-07 | 9.73 | 9.65 | 9.38 | 9.06 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | **✓** |

**Full chain (0.10 > 0.15 > 0.20 > 0.30) holds in 5 of 6 folds.**

## Decision recommendation

**Walk-fold supports trail_pct=0.05** — dominates deployed (0.30) on the (Sharpe higher AND Max DD shallower) criterion in 6 of 6 folds. Other candidates that also pass:
  - trail=0.10 (dominates in 5/6)
  - trail=0.15 (dominates in 5/6)
  - trail=0.20 (dominates in 4/6)

## Caveats (per the task brief)

1. **trail=0.05** is a boundary check; expect it to exit on noise.
2. **Time-stop frequency** — if any (fold, trail) drops below 5%, the time-stop becomes a vestigial element of the policy (see the time-stop table above). Tighter trails will increase trailing-exit and shrink time-exit toward zero.
3. **Cost realism** — shorter holds = more trades = more fees per unit P&L. The avg-cost-per-trade table tracks this; if cost rises proportionally with trade count and Sharpe gain vanishes, the dominance result reverses.
4. **Compounding artifact** — every Net P&L cell is sum of per-trade net P&L (NOT chained / NOT compounded), so early-fold results don't run away with subsequent folds via position-size growth.
5. **Same-day priority** — the harness models daily bars and resolves stop-vs-take-profit ties as stop-wins. With tighter trails this priority becomes more impactful (more bars where the trail stop and a higher close coexist on the same day). This is a known modeling limitation, not corrected here.
